case_title: DANAEE DIAZ VS. PROSEGUR SERVICES GROUP, INC., ET AL

docket_entries.description: REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT/DEFAULT ENTERED, DECLARATION OF NON-MILITARY STATUS (TRANSACTION ID # 100201025) AS TO DEFENDANT PROSEGUR SERVICES GROUP, INC., A NEW YORK CORPORATION
OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE (TRANSACTION ID # 100213658) FILED BY PLAINTIFF DIAZ, DANAEE

LAW AND MOTION 302, RE: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. NOT ARGUED/CONTESTED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO VACATE THE DEFAULT ENTERED ON MAY 24, 2023 IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THAT IT LACKED ACTUAL NOTICE OF THIS ACTION IN TIME TO DEFEND OR THAT THE DEFAULT WAS ENTERED DUE TO MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THE CAMPBELL DECLARATION CONCLUSORILY DECLARES THAT CSC, DEFENDANT'S REGISTERED AGENT FOR PROCESS, NEVER PROVIDED NOTICE OF THE ACTION TO DEFENDANT. EVEN IF THE COURT ADMITS THIS DECLARATION, THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT DEFENDANT DID IN FACT RECEIVE NOTICE. DEBONIS WORKS FOR CSC AND HER DECLARATION AND EXHIBITS DEMONSTRATE THAT THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT WERE TRANSFERRED TO DEFENDANT AND ITS EMPLOYEE (PAMELA MELENDEZ) REVIEWED THEM. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU) ***AFTER HEARING, AN ORDER IS SIGNED.

Plaintiffs Application And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Default Court Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-06-2023 Off Calendar

Motion To Reconsider Order Denying Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-27-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ANN FROMHOLZ, Esq. of The Fromholz Firm PLC (Tel: 626-568-8005) for defendant Proseguir Services Group, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT PROSEGUR SERVICES GROUP, INC.'s Motion To Reconsider Order Denying Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for reconsideration is granted.

Defendant presents new evidence showing that it in fact had actual notice of the lawsuit but due to inadvertence/excusable neglect, the papers did not reach the proper person to respond to the complaint. (CCP 473; CCP 1008; Campbell and Fromholz Decls.) Defendant was diligent and did not apprise the court of this new information because due to a personal emergency, defense counsel could not appear at the hearing on the motion to vacate.

In light of this new evidence, the motion to vacate default is granted. The court has a policy to hear cases on the merits and slight evidence is necessary to obtain relief from a default. (Miller v. City of Hermosa Beach (1993) 13 Cal.App.4th 1118, 1136.) Numerous courts have granted relief in circumstances similar to this, i.e., where a corporate employee misplaces the summons and complaint and the corporation does not timely respond to the complaint. (Pelegrinelli v. McCloud River Lumber Co. (1905) 1 Cal.App. 593 [vacating default where president of corporation put summons and complaint in his drawer and did not forward them to the insurer].)

Pursuant to CCP 473(c)(1)(C), defendant shall pay $1,000 in fees and costs to plaintiff by December 31, 2023, as reasonable expenses for obtaining the default. (Rogalski v. Nabers Cadillac (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 816, 822 ["Section 473 allows the court to impose just conditions in setting aside a default. Such conditions might include the attorney fees and costs incurred by the plaintiff in obtaining the default judgment.")

Defendant shall file its proposed answer by December 8, 2023.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-03-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Defendant's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

Appearances (via Zoom):

Ali Schalchi, Schalchi Burch LLP, (949) 359-0334, for Plaintiff.

Not argued/contested.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant's motion to vacate the default entered on May 24, 2023 is denied. Defendant fails to demonstrate that it lacked actual notice of this action in time to defend or that the default was entered due to mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. The Campbell declaration conclusorily declares that CSC, defendant's registered agent for process, never provided notice of the action to defendant. Even if the court admits this declaration, the weight of the evidence shows that defendant did in fact receive notice. DeBonis works for CSC and her declaration and exhibits demonstrate that the summons and complaint were transferred to defendant and its employee (Pamela Melendez) reviewed them.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

***After hearing, an order is signed.

Defendant has a Motion to Set Aside Default pending in efiling and that the motion is timely.

Good cause appearing, the Court continues the matter to October 25, 2023 in department 505 at 1:30 pm to allow Defendants Motion to be filed.
